scope.identity: RichEPub (.repub) toolchain — build, read, watch, and planning-pack embed. Distributed as @portfolio/repub-builder with bin aliases `repub-build` and `repub`. Installable from a GitHub tag: `pnpm add git+https://github.com/MagicbornStudios/repub-builder.git#main`.
scope.primary-consumers: Magicborn book projects (magicborn-rune-path, mordreds-tale, mordreds-legacy) and any Vite + React + Tailwind project that targets the .repub container format.

functional.requirement: `repub-build` / `repub` CLI, invoked in a project dir with vite.config.ts and dist/, produces a single .repub ZIP containing the manifest (repub.json), entry HTML, and bundled assets. Flags: --skip-install, --skip-build.

Export `@portfolio/repub-builder/reader` so host apps can mount the reader component inside their own React trees, with opt-in planning strip via showPlanningStrip prop (decision captured in commit 03399df).

Export `@portfolio/repub-builder/planning-pack` — serialize a project's GAD planning docs (STATE / ROADMAP / TASK-REGISTRY / DECISIONS) into a reader-embeddable pack. Manifest format in planning-pack-manifest.ts.
`repub watch` — debounced directory-triggered command runner (feature landed in commit c2b3443). Rebuilds on content change.
mdxToHtml.ts converts chapter MDX into reader-ready HTML with frontmatter preserved.

non-functional.requirement: Package is published neither to npm nor anywhere mutable — consumers pin a GitHub tag. Repo owns its own release story.
Source in src/, built via tsup (tsup.config.ts) to dist/. ESM.
Unit tests colocated (epub.test.ts, others); vitest.config.ts at repo root. CI expectation: `pnpm test` green before release tag.
CLI must run under Windows without Unix-only path assumptions.

out-of-scope.item: Hosting a public reader webapp — host apps mount the reader themselves.
.repub signing or DRM — pack-only, no rights enforcement.
Cloud build service — CLI is invoked locally or by CI.

open-questions.question: Does the reader runtime stay in this repo long-term, or split into a sibling package once a second consumer emerges?
Planning-pack schema evolution — versioning story when the pack format changes under host apps that have shipped an older reader. Migration hook? Pack-version negotiation?
CI — does the upstream repo need a GitHub Actions workflow to run `pnpm test` + `pnpm build` on push, or is contributor discipline sufficient at this scale?
